core_meaning: Expressing strong emotion about something using quel before noun
primary_communicative_fn: Conveying surprise, admiration, or strong emotion about a quality or entity
when_to_use: When reacting emotionally to a noun or its quality; when expressing admiration or surprise about something
essential_vocabulary_slots: quel, quelle, quels, quelles, beau, belle, bon, surprise, enfant, nouvelle
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting to agree quel with noun: *« quel beaux » instead of « quels beaux »
Using adjective before quel: *« beaux quel temps »
primary_function: Expressing admiration or surprise about nouns and their qualities
key_forms: Quel beau temps! Quelle belle surprise! Quels beaux enfants! Quelles bonnes nouvelles!
essential_vocabulary: quel, quelle, quels, quelles, beau, belle, bon, surprise, enfant, nouvelle
why_it_matters: Essential for expressing genuine reactions and emotions in French; builds emotional expressiveness needed in conversation
common_mistakes: Forgetting gender/number agreement on quel; incorrect word order (adjective before quel)

core_meaning: Exclamation with que fronting the verb phrase for emphasis on action or state
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasizing intensity or degree of an action or quality through fronted exclamation
when_to_use: When expressing intense emotion about an action, state, or quality; when emphasizing how much/how greatly something is true
pattern: Que + [sujet + verbe] + ! → emphase sur l'action ou l'état
essential_vocabulary_slots: que, sujet, verbe, faim, gentil, faire, beau, content
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing que (exclamation) with qui/que (relative pronoun)
Forgetting the subject after que: *« Que fait beau! »
chunk_compatibility: Exclamative chunks: *Que c'est beau!*, *Que c'est cher!*, *Qu'il est gentil!*
primary_function: Expressing intensity or degree of emotion about actions/states
key_forms: Que j'ai faim! Que tu es gentil! Qu'il fait beau! Que nous sommes contents!
essential_vocabulary: que, avoir, faim, être, gentil, faire, beau, content, tremper
why_it_matters: Enables natural emotional expression; essential for fluent, expressive speech in daily French
common_mistakes: Confusing exclamatory que with interrogative or relative que; omitting subject after que

core_meaning: Exclamation emphasizing degree with comme meaning 'how' or expressing comparison of intensity
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing the degree or intensity of a quality, action, or state with emphasis on 'how much'
when_to_use: When commenting on the degree or intensity of something; when expressing admiration for how much or how greatly something is true
pattern: Comme + sujet + verbe + ! → souligner le degré d'une action ou qualité
essential_vocabulary_slots: comme, sujet, verbe, faire, beau, grandir, heureux, courir, vite
common_learner_mistakes: Using « comment » instead of « comme »: *« comment il fait beau »
Incorrect subject placement after comme: *« Comme fait il beau »
chunk_compatibility: Exclamative chunks: *Comme c'est joli!*, *Comme tu as raison!*, *Comme nous sommes chanceux!*
primary_function: Emphasizing degree or intensity; expressing How much or how greatly
key_forms: Comme il fait beau! Comme tu as grandi! Comme nous sommes heureux! Comme elle court vite!
essential_vocabulary: comme, faire, beau, avoir, grandir, être, heureux, courir, vite, joli
why_it_matters: Natural conversational marker for expressing degree and admiration; essential for sounding native-like in casual speech
common_mistakes: Confusing « comment » (how in questions) with « comme » (how in exclamations); wrong subject-verb placement

core_meaning: Cleft sentence emphasizing a quality or fact through restructuring to highlight what matters most
primary_communicative_fn: Focusing and emphasizing a specific element of a sentence by moving it to the front with ce que/c'est
when_to_use: When emphasizing what you like, want, or value most; when highlighting the most important part of a statement
key_variations: Ce que j'aime, c'est le chocolat.
Ce qu'il fait beau, c'est formidable.
Ce que je veux, c'est partir.
Ce qui nous plaît, c'est votre courage.
essential_vocabulary_slots: ce, que, c'est, aimer, chocolat, vouloir, partir, plaire, courage
common_learner_mistakes: Omitting the second part (c'est): *« ce que j'aime » without continuation
Using « ce qui » when ce que is correct (and vice versa); subject/object confusion
chunk_compatibility: Cleft emphasis chunks: *Ce que je veux, c'est...*, *Ce qu'il faut, c'est...*, *Ce qui manque, c'est...*
primary_function: Emphasizing and focusing particular elements of sentences
key_forms: Ce que j'aime, c'est le chocolat. Ce qu'il faut, c'est du courage. Ce qui compte, c'est la santé.
essential_vocabulary: ce, que, c'est, aimer, vouloir, falloir, compter, courage, santé, chocolat
why_it_matters: Advanced structure for natural emphasis in French; allows sophisticated highlighting of key ideas
common_mistakes: Mixing ce que/ce qui; incomplete cleft (missing c'est clause); subject/object pronoun confusion

core_meaning: Exclamation expressing surprise, admiration, or strong emotion about a direct object or situation
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing surprised or emphatic reaction to a direct object, action, or situation
when_to_use: When reacting with surprise or strong emotion to something someone did or said; when emphasizing the intensity of an action
pattern: Qu'est-ce que + sujet + verbe + ! → réaction exclamatory à un complément d'objet
essential_vocabulary_slots: qu'est-ce que, sujet, verbe, beau, manger, réussir, rire, c'est
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing interrogative and exclamative uses (same form, context differs)
Using « qu'est-ce qui » for direct object: should use « qu'est-ce que »
chunk_compatibility: Exclamative chunks: *Qu'est-ce que c'est beau!*, *Qu'est-ce que tu es brave!*, *Qu'est-ce qu'il a grandi!*
primary_function: Expressing surprise or emphatic reaction to direct objects
key_forms: Qu'est-ce que c'est beau! Qu'est-ce que tu es brave! Qu'est-ce qu'il a grandi!
essential_vocabulary: qu'est-ce que, être, beau, brave, grandir, c'est, réussir, manger, rire
why_it_matters: Natural exclamatory structure essential for expressing surprise and emphasis in conversational French
common_mistakes: Mixing interrogative and exclamative contexts; confusing qu'est-ce que (object) with qu'est-ce qui (subject)

core_meaning: Simple exclamations using interjections (ah, oh, eh, hélas, merde) with punctuation to express emotion
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing immediate emotional reactions, surprise, disappointment, or acknowledgment
when_to_use: In casual conversation to express surprise, pleasure, disappointment, or to acknowledge understanding
essential_vocabulary_slots: ah, oh, eh, hélas, merde, bien, là, zut, non, oublié
common_learner_mistakes: Overusing interjections in formal writing (too colloquial)
Confusing meaning: « eh » is attention-getter, « oh » is surprise, « ah » is realization
chunk_compatibility: Interjection chunks: *Ah bon!*, *Oh là là!*, *Eh bien!*, *Hélas, non!*, *Merde!*
primary_function: Expressing immediate emotional reactions and acknowledgments
key_forms: Ah! Ah, c'est bien! Oh là là! Eh bien! Hélas, non! Merde!
essential_vocabulary: ah, oh, eh, hélas, merde, zut, bon, flûte, non, oui
why_it_matters: Essential for natural, spontaneous speech; adds authenticity and emotional depth to French conversation
common_mistakes: Overusing in formal contexts; misinterpreting interjection meanings; incorrect punctuation after

structure_label: Dire que... (exclamative of surprise at reality)
core_meaning: Exclamation expressing surprise or strong reaction that something is (or isn't) the case
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing surprise, disbelief, or emotional reaction to a situation or fact
when_to_use: When reacting with surprise or dismay to something that has happened or a situation that exists
pattern: Dire que + indicatif → souligner la surprise face à une réalité
key_variations: Dire que j'ai cru que ça n'arriverait jamais!
Dire que nous étions si inquiets!
essential_vocabulary_slots: dire, que, croire, arriver, minuit, être, inquiet, réussir, jamais
common_learner_mistakes: Using conditional instead of indicative: *« Dire que j'aurais... »
Confusing with « dire » as verb meaning 'to say' (to introduce speech)
chunk_compatibility: Exclamative chunks: *Dire que c'est déjà fini!*, *Dire qu'on ne s'en est pas rendu compte!*
primary_function: Expressing surprise or strong emotion at an unexpected fact or situation
Expressing surprise or emotional reaction to surprising facts
key_forms: Dire qu'il est déjà minuit! Dire que tu as réussi! Dire qu'on ne s'en est pas rendu compte!
essential_vocabulary: dire, que, minuit, réussir, rendre compte, croire, arriver, inquiet, jamais
why_it_matters: Authentic French expression of surprise and emotion; shows emotional engagement with ideas and events
common_mistakes: Using wrong tense (conditional instead of indicative); confusing with speech verb dire

core_meaning: Exclamation expressing sudden awareness or unexpected realization of something
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing sudden surprise or realization that something has happened or is the case
when_to_use: When suddenly realizing something or becoming aware of a changed situation
pattern: Soudain que + indicatif → surprendre avec une réalisation soudaine
key_variations: Soudain qu'on est déjà en mai!
essential_vocabulary_slots: soudain, que, faire, froid, partir, changer, être, mai, froid
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with concessive « encore que »
Using with future tense (soudain only works with present/past): *« Soudain qu'il fera... »
chunk_compatibility: Realization chunks: *Soudain qu'on n'a plus le temps!*, *Soudain qu'il fait nuit!*
primary_function: Expressing sudden realization or awareness of a changed situation
key_forms: Soudain qu'il fait froid! Soudain qu'elle est partie! Soudain que tout a changé!
essential_vocabulary: soudain, que, faire, froid, partir, être, changer, mai, nuit, temps
why_it_matters: Expresses natural conversational patterns of sudden awareness; authentic in narrative and spoken French
common_mistakes: Using with future tense; confusing with concessive clauses; incorrect tense usage

core_meaning: Informal emphatic affirmative meaning 'and how!' or 'you bet!' expressing strong yes or certainty
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing emphatic affirmation or certainty in response to a question in very colloquial speech
when_to_use: In very casual, colloquial speech when affirming strongly or emphatically that something is true or will happen
pattern: Un peu que + indicatif/conditionnel! → affirmation emphatique familière
key_variations: Un peu que ça fait mal!
Un peu que il va venir!
essential_vocabulary_slots: peu, que, avoir, faim, faire, mal, aller, venir, être, content
common_learner_mistakes: Using in formal contexts: too colloquial and informal for standard French
Confusing with « un peu de » meaning 'some' (different structure entirely)
chunk_compatibility: Colloquial affirmation chunks: *Un peu que oui!*, *Un peu que tu vas voir!*
primary_function: Emphatic affirmation in very informal, colloquial speech
key_forms: Un peu que j'ai faim! Un peu que ça fait mal! Un peu que tu vas voir!
essential_vocabulary: peu, que, avoir, faim, faire, mal, aller, venir, être, oui, content
why_it_matters: Important for understanding and participating in very casual French conversation; shows register awareness
common_mistakes: Using in formal contexts; confusing with « un peu de »; incorrect register

core_meaning: Exclamation strongly denying something, meaning 'far from it!' or expressing emphatic negation
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing emphatic negation or contradiction to a stated or implied idea
when_to_use: When strongly denying a proposition or correcting a misunderstanding about the truth or extent of something
pattern: Tant s'en faut que + subjonctif! → négation emphatique et formelle
key_variations: Tant s'en faut que ce soit facile!
Tant s'en faut que je sois d'accord!
Tant s'en faut qu'il ait réussi!
Tant s'en faut que ce soit terminé!
essential_vocabulary_slots: tant, faut, que, être, facile, accord, réussir, terminer, sois
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting the subjunctive after tant s'en faut: *« Tant s'en faut qu'il est »
Confusing positive/negative context or scope of negation
chunk_compatibility: Emphatic negation chunks: *Tant s'en faut que j'accepte!*, *Tant s'en faut qu'on soit prêt!*
primary_function: Expressing emphatic negation and strong contradiction
key_forms: Tant s'en faut que ce soit facile! Tant s'en faut que je sois d'accord! Tant s'en faut qu'il soit venu!
essential_vocabulary: tant, faut, que, être, facile, accord, réussir, terminer, subjonctif, venir
why_it_matters: Formal, emphatic structure essential for sophisticated disagreement and contradiction in French
common_mistakes: Forgetting subjunctive mood; incorrect scope of negation; confusion in literary vs. conversational contexts

core_meaning: Exclamation saying something is not as much or as great as one might think; de-emphasizing or downplaying
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing that something is less extreme, difficult, or significant than it appears or might be thought
when_to_use: When downplaying difficulty, importance, or degree of something; when moderating expectations
pattern: Pas si + adjectif/adverbe + que + phrase/ça → minimiser ou relativiser
key_variations: Pas si facile que vous croyez!
essential_vocabulary_slots: pas, si, difficile, facile, mal, vite, que, ça, croire
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting second « que »: *« pas si difficile » alone is adjective, not exclamative
Confusing with simple comparative (missing second element of comparison)
chunk_compatibility: De-emphasis chunks: *Pas si grave!*, *Pas si important que vous croyez!*
primary_function: De-emphasizing difficulty, importance, or degree; moderating expectations
key_forms: Pas si difficile que ça! Pas si facile que vous croyez! Pas si mal que ça!
essential_vocabulary: pas, si, difficile, facile, mal, vite, que, ça, grave, important, croire
why_it_matters: Essential for nuanced conversation; allows sophisticated downplaying and perspective-shifting in French
common_mistakes: Missing second « que »; confusing with simple adjective; treating as pure comparative

core_meaning: Exclamation pointing out something with emotional emphasis, often illustrating a consequence or example
primary_communicative_fn: Pointing out examples or consequences with emotional emphasis and commentary
when_to_use: When illustrating a point through example; when commenting on consequences of actions or situations
pattern: Voilà ce que + [clause complète] + ! → pointer avec emphase émotionnelle
key_variations: Voilà ce que c'est que de trop parler!
Voilà ce que j'appelle un bon repas!
Voilà ce que c'est la vie!
Voilà ce que peut accomplir la persévérance!
essential_vocabulary_slots: voilà, ce, que, c'est, parler, appeler, bon, repas, vie, persévérance
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with « voici » (proximity) vs « voilà » (distance/generality)
Using « voilà que » which has different meaning (expressing suddenness)
chunk_compatibility: Illustrative chunks: *Voilà ce que je veux dire!*, *Voilà ce que tu risques!*
primary_function: Pointing out examples and consequences with emotional emphasis
key_forms: Voilà ce que c'est que de trop parler! Voilà ce que j'appelle un bon repas!
essential_vocabulary: voilà, ce, que, c'est, appeler, parler, repas, vie, persévérance, risquer
why_it_matters: Natural way to illustrate points and comment on situations; essential for storytelling and explanation
common_mistakes: Confusing voilà/voici distinction; mixing with voilà que structure; wrong emphasis

core_meaning: Euphemistic oath expressions from older French literary tradition, now archaic or humorous
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing strong emotion or surprise using archaic oaths; historically authentic in literature
when_to_use: In literary contexts, historical fiction, or humorous/nostalgic speech; rarely in modern conversation
essential_vocabulary_slots: jarnicot, palsambleu, parbleu, cordieu, surprise, vrai, magnifique, alors
common_learner_mistakes: Using in modern context: these are archaic/humorous only
Not recognizing them as exclamations in literary texts; misinterpreting archaic swearing
primary_function: Expressing surprise/emotion using archaic oaths; recognizing literary authenticity
key_forms: Jarnicot! Palsambleu! Parbleu, c'est vrai! Cordieu, quel malheur!
essential_vocabulary: jarnicot, palsambleu, parbleu, cordieu, zest, surprise, vrai, magnifique, malheur
why_it_matters: Essential for understanding French literature and historical texts; shows cultural and literary awareness
common_mistakes: Using in modern contexts; not recognizing as archaic; misinterpreting historical swearing

core_meaning: Mild archaic exclamations showing surprise, frustration, or mild annoyance; euphemisms for stronger swearing
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing mild surprise, frustration, or annoyance using polite euphemistic oaths
when_to_use: In literary texts, nostalgic or humorous speech; sometimes in deliberate retro or comic contexts
essential_vocabulary_slots: fichtre, sapristi, flûte, zut, embêtant, délai, oublier, alors
common_learner_mistakes: Using in formal speech (too colloquial)
Not knowing these are euphemisms for stronger swearing; misinterpreting register
primary_function: Expressing mild frustration or surprise with polite euphemistic oaths
key_forms: Fichtre! Sapristi! Flûte, j'ai oublié! Zut, alors!
essential_vocabulary: fichtre, sapristi, flûte, zut, embêtant, délai, oublier, alors, diantre
why_it_matters: Shows register awareness and understanding of euphemism in French; recognizable in literature and culture
common_mistakes: Using in formal contexts; misunderstanding euphemistic nature; wrong register application

core_meaning: Expression meaning 'in abundance, as much as you want' or 'and plenty of it'; expressing plentiful supply
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing abundance, plenty, or more than enough of something with emphatic casualness
when_to_use: When describing something that exists in large quantities; when emphasizing abundance or availability
pattern: [Nom] + , en veux-tu, en voilà → expression d'abondance
key_variations: Il y avait en veux-tu, en voilà de bons restaurants.
Des conseils, en veux-tu, en voilà.
En veux-tu, en voilà des raisons de s'inquiéter!
Il y en a en veux-tu, en voilà!
essential_vocabulary_slots: veux, voilà, veux-tu, voilà, conseil, raison, restaurant, abondance
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with other expressions of quantity
Using as question: this is always exclamatory statement, never interrogative
chunk_compatibility: Abundance chunks: *En veux-tu, en voilà de problèmes!*, *Des raisons, en veux-tu, en voilà!*
primary_function: Expressing abundance and plenty with emphatic casualness
key_forms: Des conseils, en veux-tu, en voilà. En veux-tu, en voilà des raisons! Il y en a en veux-tu, en voilà!
essential_vocabulary: veux, voilà, conseil, raison, restaurant, abondance, problème, s'inquiéter
why_it_matters: Authentic French idiom for expressing abundance; essential for natural, idiomatic speech
common_mistakes: Treating as interrogative; confusing with other quantity expressions; wrong word order
